Looks good. Beautiful. So this is the exclusively empowered early access. At a high level, what are we doing while we are trying to get that consent and permission for our launch? And when it is given, when they are given consent to participate in our launch via a link click, we are going to use that to trigger both an early access email and or an interstitial page or a redirect to an early version of the sales page where they are going to get. That exclusively empowered offer via an early third coupon code. So what is actually going on here? What are we trying to achieve within this email? We're trying to achieve some belief shifting content. So this is where we're bringing those sacred cows that we've spoken about in 10 X launches into the foray. Right. What are we having them buy into that relates to the offer? What is the transformation where hard selling is that we could soft sell the actual offer? We are inserting that where possible. Most importantly, we're trying to get consent via binary choice, right? Offering a very clear yes or no around participating in the launch and qualifying. What that means? Who should be a yes for participating? Who should be a no and not in a weird shaming way, but in a legitimate no, this might not be the best use of your attention over the next few days. Getting that yes, we are achieving and I hate the term still a mid crow commitment. So not a micro commitment where they, you know, I don't know, put their name on an opt-in form and have momentum going that way. But an actual commitment that is in line with what the final yes would be. Right. So if someone is saying a clear yes that for XYZ reasons, they're interested in hearing about the offer and participating in the launch, that is a yes that correlates with the final yes. So there's momentum going in the right direction. And then we have the exclusively empowered offer, right? So we have an element of positive surprise where they're not expecting that early access. They just raised their hand and said, yes, I'm interested in this. And now they have this positive momentum, this sense of positive surprise and this exclusively empowered offer where they get both priority pricing and early access to the program that they might be getting a little bit excited about. And what this all does, why we're doing it from an organizational standpoint is to offer is an opportunity started to generate rapid momentum before the main cart even opened, right? Launch weeks, I don't know, they tend to be a little bit stressful. That's what I keep hearing over the years. But when you could open it with a bang, right? When you could have this early influx of sales before the main cart has even opened, it just changes the whole dynamic of that launch itself. It gives you new confidence. It gives your team confidence. It gives you cash flow if you're investing in retargeting ads. It's just overall a good place to be. Yeah, so you had like three options here, right? So I think it wasn't just binary choice, but there were essentially two forms of yeses that drove to the sales page. Yeah, so great, great call out. So one of the aspects of this launch was we have active members of two different programs that we wanted to, and this is an ongoing process of migrating these audiences into this new ecosystem. And so we really wanted to honor them with a really nice offer to kind of come up board early in a way that would be meaningful to them. So that was kind of option number one. Option number two was for folks that weren't part of those memberships and they just really wanted to kind of get in early on this lifetime deal that we were offering. And then obviously number three was the folks that, yeah, for whatever reason, weren't resonating, weren't ready or interested in exploring the offer and yeah, just letting them get back to their normal scheduled programming. So those are kind of our three groups there. Cool. And yeah, as we mentioned, we'll show you in the automations essentially how to mute people out of those sequences when they opt out of to really kind of maintain the respect of your list and honoring that no, instead of just pummeling them with emails after they said they want out. But essentially the mechanism of this for you guys was these two yes links essentially drove to an interstitial or drove straight to an early access version of the sales page. So for option number one, it drove to an interstitial and there was like a higher touch process of communication that we did there with the members. Because we really wanted to make sure things were clear. Option number two, in this case. Now I could see cause to do it both ways. And this cause for or in this case for simplicity, we actually drove straight to the sales page. So that's what we chose to do. And then yeah, with number three, just took them to kind of a yeah, just the page that said, hey, we're confirming your, you know, your request so that they know exactly anything else. Yep. So there's that element of surprise, right? There once again, they're not expecting to have access to that offer yet. So it's very clear that they've unlocked that exclusively empowered offer. And then yeah, just a short email, just kind of like filling them in on those details. Cool. So to wrap this up, I really just want to give you two additional examples. So the first one, this was actually from Coffee School Black Friday 2020. So this was a bit of a different motivation for using this one. This was like essentially knowing that everyone's inboxes would be absolutely flooded with Black Friday offers and wanting to kind of get a jump on that. Right? So this was both kind of like the getting permission to participate in it and being like, Hey, you said you want to participate. Why not just like wrap it up right now and you could feel good about getting that epic deal and not get caught up in all the crazy hypeness that's going to flood your inbox. And your consciousness in two days from now. So yeah, here were some subject lines we played with read now before she gets real. And then essentially the bulk of this email was about Black Friday madness. So this was much more seasonal than it was, you know, sacred cows and belief reshifting. This is really offer related because it was a Black Friday deal. So we said overheard on just about every marketer slack channel marketer. Hey, so Black Friday is coming up. What should we do? Our thing be, I don't know. Let's just take some random piece of content, logic and module for that program. No one ever got results in and hype the living hell, sinky out of it. Let's do this with all due respect to Finland and his holiness. Neil deGrasse. Neil deGrasse Tyson. Am I getting there right? We absolutely will not be doing this now. We'll be disembarking the hype train and taking a SpaceX shuttle straight to the planet of OMFG, Arjo and Co actually doing this, doing what you ask. Then we talk about the offer that we're going to be releasing. Plus some epic surprises, all for less than a weekly case of Heineken. In short, we're not going to waste your precious time, energy, attention on some suboptimal deal that you snag and forget about by the time Michael Boublet's Christmas album croons from the depths of your living in Sonos. And so we're going to give you the best of what we got at the best price ever and make you feel damn good about securing a training package that you'll reference literally every time you open a fresh Google Doc to write that ad or sale sequence or sales page that in no uncertain language will declare what you will or won't get to whiskey clink to on your next team. Zoom call. And then we have kind of our binary choice. If you want to be shoulder tapped the second this copy school package becomes available, click here to let me know on the other hand, if you'd prefer to save your attention and black Friday dollars for the product equivalent of Sharknado for is that for Roman numerals? Yeah, V is five. Cool. Plucked out of a Walmart discount DVD bin. Let me know here and I'll make sure to put you on the do not disturb list of the release literally our best offer ever. No, it's OK. Really tune it out. It's fine. So maybe this is a little bit more in the like playful. I wouldn't call this a shaming off that link. But yeah, if your tone is fun and you say things like that, why not? Right. But this is really the link. This is really the click we're after. So it's very clear that someone clicking this is confirming that they are interested in this package. There's almost an open loop and a curiosity and an excitement to find out what it is. And the most powerful kind of open loop in your marketing is one where people actually are really curious and interested and excited to know what your offer is. So imagine the excitement where you think you're just clicking to be notified when that happens in the next two or three days and boom, almost immediately, you get the opportunity to actually buy it. There's just a form of momentum that results in that early conversion. And then if you want to know what the triggered email looks like, this is what we had. I'm going to help you up with a pre-sale link over here. And then, yeah, that's really their exclusive empowered offer. And that's what it looked like for something seasonal and very offer focused like a Black Friday deal for something that is a little bit more of the belief shifting content. We had this version, which went out for our CTC platinum affiliate launch. So this is something that an affiliate sent promoting coaching the conversion platinum. And this object was a robot wrote this and essentially the whole belief free shift here was that copywriting isn't something that the bots can do. And this is something we were kind of responding to because there were so many kind of like AI writing software is coming out that we're going all crazy about. And we thought it would be a good conversation to enter. So everyone's freaking out about freaking out about this whole AI copybot craze, which is just weird to me. Like, how is this even a conversation unless you still consider copywriting to be a list of formulaic power words have zero respect for your audience's intelligence or sophistication level, believe that a bot and it's still shouldn't itself infancy can outperform a fully matured human. It's not even a relevant question pinning a bot and gains a well trained copywriter sounds more like an awkward carnival side show than a legitimate showdown. Kind of like the UFC's early days when they pin black belt and gains boxers for the sheer hilarity of it all. Much like those early UFC days, it's simply a different game, different discipline, apples versus lab engineered toy burgers. Because here's the thing, copywriting has evolved way beyond being able to write words that persuade has become the multi-layered art and skill of, and I go through all the things that I believe copywriting to be that is in alignment with what our offer provides for better or worse. You'll never be able to populate a bunch of stuff into a box called your audience's deepest fears and have a machine spit out anything more than a placeholder. AI copybot versus copywriter is only a valid battle in non-relationship driven businesses. Maybe they can square off in a product description for a door hinge or the label ingredients on a jar of organic pasture farm chipotle or supplement that cleanses your gut of soy burger residue. But when you try to replace a copywriter, what you're really trying to do is take over a rich deep nuanced relationship with ever changing dynamics. Asking a bot to take over that relationship is no different than asking a bot to parent your kids or connect with your partner. This is extra true if you're a coach consultant or expert whose business is literally built on trusted relationships and deep resonance. So we go through essentially the fact that they are negotiating this deal on coaching to conversion platinum. It's not out yet. Essentially, if you want to get priority access to the private deal, we've locked in for you. Click here to join Team Human. So this one didn't have the opt out link, but it essentially had the call to action to the shoulder tap once it goes live. And then, yes, the triggered email very similar to the copy hackers version. Essentially, you now have the ability to do it. You've unlocked it and you have a code that will give you extra savings. So that is what I wanted to share with you guys on that one. Obviously, there are different variations and formats for using it. Even speaking about the exclusively empowered offer element, all three of those examples we broke down were entirely different. The Black Friday deal was the same as the one that went live. The exclusively empowered element was just that they got early access to it. They got to seal the deal right then and there. And for many, that was exciting enough for CTC Platinum. I think it was a $200 discount code on a $1,000 product. So it's actually 20% off for Phil's client. It was actually a much greater discount. I think it was like 50% off of the total ticket price. So you know, it was like, you know, it was like 100 off of, it was 100 off essentially off of $297. So that was already discounted down, right? So like we're already selling that higher now that we're off of the launch. Yep. So there are many different dynamics you can explore for that. The main crux of this strategy is just that surprise early access to the offer in some way, shape, or form that gives them an incentive for completing it. Even if that incentive is beating Black Friday madness and just getting it done now and enjoying early access to it before anyone else even knows the offer is live. For many, that is exciting in and of itself. So I think that's all I want to say on that strategy. It's something that like has been a revelation for me. It sounds like it's been amazing for your client and your ability to kind of generate initial momentum there. And yeah, when it comes to attention spans, shrinking, and really honoring the return on attention, return on opening an email, and rewarding someone with an opportunity for it, I think it just plays so well into just how audiences are consuming with and engaging in launches in late 2021 as of now, 2022 and beyond. So that one is that. I encourage you all to use it and we'll meet you in the next video where we're going to go over the mid-heart temperature check. Thanks, Phil. Yep. Take care.
